prd_ref: PRD §6 Coordinated Cluster Case / §7 Eval C
title: Coordinated cluster converging on a single table
fixture_note: Operator-facing demo-case scenario. Structure + entity references only — NO computed scores or classifications (P3 produces those). convergent_signals / soft_play_delta values are simulated fields (seeded ground truth), restated here as the demo narrative target — never real detection.
narrative: Accounts A (P-198) and B (P-199) already sit at T-11 and are device-linked via DG-001. Account C (P-200) requests the open third seat, which would complete an A/B/C co-seating formation. Multiple WEAK signals converge: device link (A/B), timing correlation (C→A 0.88, C→B 0.85), repeated co-seating (14 of 18 recent tables), within-cluster soft-play (negative soft_play_delta), and outsider-pressure driving casuals out. No single signal proves collusion — convergence is the signal. The safe action is to HOLD the third seat for pit-boss review before the formation completes. Recommend review — never accuse, never auto-enforce.

demo_spine.lobby: Player lobby is unaffected and shows nothing integrity-related (player-facing separation is load-bearing).
demo_spine.pit_boss: Operator console surfaces integrity score, convergent signals, counter-evidence, AI summary, and the hold recommendation. Operator actions: accept containment / override / escalate to manual review.
demo_spine.simulator: Standard: C completes the formation, cluster plays as a unit, casual-exit impact high. FairPlay: C held → formation blocked → pit boss accepts containment → C re-routed.
demo_spine.eval: Eval C checks the cluster ranks high on integrity convergence, the third seat is held, ALL counter-evidence is surfaced, and NO auto-ban/enforcement is recommended.

divergence_points.standard: Account C (P-200) seated at T-11 → A/B/C co-seating formation complete
divergence_points.fairplay: Third seat held for pit-boss review → formation incomplete

divergence_points.standard: No review pending; formation already entrenched
divergence_points.fairplay: Pit boss accepts containment → C re-routed to a separate table → formation blocked

expected_outcome.standard: Cluster formation completes; coordinated unit drives casual exits; high_risk_seating_formations stays elevated.
expected_outcome.fairplay: Formation blocked at review; high_risk_seating_formations reduced; casual retention preserved.

convergent_signals.note: Simulated ground-truth seeds from seeded_case_labels.json. P3 computes the real integrity packet.

counter_evidence: No single signal alone is proof of collusion.
Device link could be a shared workstation.
Timing correlation could reflect same time zone / schedule.
Co-seating could reflect a shared preference for the same stake level.
Recommended action is HUMAN REVIEW — not enforcement.
lens_separation_guard: This case is INTEGRITY_RISK. It is the true-positive integrity case and must rank ABOVE the false-positive traps (CASE-D regular overlap, CASE-E shared device). It must use uncertainty language and never state collusion as fact.
id_reconciliation: Canonical cluster IDs are P-198/P-199/P-200 (agree across players.json + seeded_case_labels.json).

table_roster.json T-11 seated_player_ids currently show P-CA/P-CB (Bram placeholder namespace) instead of P-198/P-199 — Bram must reconcile the roster seating to the canonical P-19x IDs. Flagged to Tate.
My decision_list.json was patched to P-CA/P-CB/P-CC from the roster; once the namespace is canonicalized to P-19x, decision_list triggers should be re-patched to match. Tracked in cleo-handoff.
